title: Nettle infusion for cycle support
subtitle: A mineral-rich tonic infusion for the second half of the cycle
excerpt: A daily hot infusion of dried nettle leaf, taken in the week before menstruation as a nutritive tonic for the cycle. Nettle is rich in iron, calcium, and magnesium, the minerals most depleted by menstruation, and has a long tradition as a cycle-support herb in western herbalism.
sourceNotes: Maud Grieve, A Modern Herbal (1931), nettle entry, nutritive tonic properties, haemostatic use, iron and mineral content. Traditional use of nettle as a blood and cycle tonic in European domestic medicine. EMA HMPC assessment of Urtica dioica folium (2011), confirms high mineral content including iron, magnesium, and calcium.

content.text: A daily cup of nettle leaf tea in the week before menstruation. Nettle is the herbal tradition's foremost

. Its leaves are rich in iron, calcium, magnesium, and silica, the minerals most lost during a period. A daily cup in the second half of the cycle (the week or so from ovulation to the period) tops up those minerals before they go. It may also reduce the fatigue, irritability, and low energy some women feel before a period. A tonic preparation for general support, not a treatment for any one condition.

attrs.body:
Not medical advice. Consult a medical professional for ongoing or serious symptoms.

Nettle leaf at normal tea strength is well tolerated and safe for regular use. The main interaction noted is with warfarin: nettle contains vitamin K, and very high intake (several litres a day) could in theory affect INR. One or two cups a day is unlikely to matter, but tell your prescriber if on warfarin.

Pregnancy: nettle leaf infusion is widely used in pregnancy and is considered safe at normal tea strength. It is a traditional preparation for nourishing the blood during pregnancy. As with any herb, regular use in the first trimester is best discussed with a midwife.

See a medical professional for irregular or absent periods, very heavy periods, or premenstrual symptoms that get in the way of daily life. The same goes for any change in cycle pattern. These need proper checking.
attrs.title: Important safety notes

items.name: Dried nettle leaf
items.prepNote: about 1 heaped teaspoon of dried leaf

items.name: Honey
items.prepNote: optional, nettle tea is mild and slightly earthy

content.text: Place {{dried-nettle-leaf}} of dried nettle leaf in the teapot or infuser. Pour {{water}} of just-off-boil water over and lid immediately. Steep for 10 minutes. Strain and drink. The taste is mild, green, and slightly grassy, more pleasant than many herbal teas, and well suited to daily regular drinking.

One to two cups a day through the second half of the cycle (from ovulation until the period begins). Carry on for the first few days of the period when mineral loss is at its highest. Nettle is safe for daily long-term use. The tonic effect builds up over time: most people notice the benefit after two or three cycles rather than at once.
When NOT to use this remedy

See a medical professional for changes to your menstrual cycle, heavier bleeding, missed periods, or premenstrual symptoms that are getting worse. Don't assume those will respond to a herbal tonic alone. Tell your prescriber if you are on warfarin and starting a regular nettle infusion. Nettle is a tonic, not a treatment for menstrual conditions.
